key_risks: Semi/AI sentiment reversal if mega-cap tech selling intensifies (Microsoft -2.3%, Oracle -4.6% on decision day)
Sticky CPI (+4.2% YoY headline) prompts hawkish Fed repricing, hurting duration and growth
Biotech high volatility and binary clinical/regulatory risk
Industrial/mid-cap cyclicals vulnerable if Q1 GDP softness (+1.6%) extends
Concentration in high-beta semis (beta 2.21) amplifies drawdowns

portfolio.rationale: Micron's blowout Q3 results ($41B rev vs $24B prior) and Q4 guide to $50B with 86% gross margin signal extraordinary memory/HBM cycle strength benefiting the whole semi complex.
Manufacturing PMI at 55.7 with output 57.7 shows strong industrial expansion; sector has solid momentum with moderate volatility.
Mid-caps benefit from broadening breadth, falling 10Y yields (4.40%), and domestic growth; reasonable volatility profile.
Strong recent catalyst-driven momentum with falling rates supportive of biotech financing; healthcare sector also showing strength.
10Y dropped to 4.40% from 4.50%; oil -3.9% and gold -3.4% suggest disinflation impulse that supports duration into the window.

portfolio_rationale: Tilts toward semis on Micron's blockbuster guide signaling memory/AI cycle acceleration, paired with cyclical industrials/mid-caps benefiting from strong PMIs and broadening breadth, plus duration as a hedge given falling yields and commodity deflation.
rationale_summary: Concentrated in semis (Micron catalyst), supported by strong manufacturing PMI cyclicals and mid-caps, with biotech and long Treasuries adding diversification amid falling yields.
